body:
### Describe the bug

When attempting to compile a Cython module living in a package that contains a Python module with the same name as the module, I get seemingly erroneous error messages about missing 'pxd' files.  This worked fine in Cython 0.29.32 but no longer works in Cython 3.2.4.  I'm not sure if this is at all related to https://github.com/cython/cython/issues/6458.

This can easily be reproduced with a few mostly empty files.  In this case, I have a package directory containing 4 files: the Python module of the same name, an `__init__.py`, the Cython module in question, and a pxd file exposing some C functions.

### Code to reproduce the behaviour:

```bash
> tail -n +1 ../CythonTest/*
==> ../CythonTest/CythonTest.py <==

==> ../CythonTest/__init__.py <==

==> ../CythonTest/OtherCython.pyx <==
from . cimport some_c_library

==> ../CythonTest/some_c_library.pxd <==

> python3 -m cython -I /home/mcnally/tmp/CythonTest/ OtherCython.pyx 

Error compiling Cython file:
------------------------------------------------------------
...
from . cimport some_c_library
^
------------------------------------------------------------
OtherCython.pyx:1:0: 'CythonTest.pxd' not found
```


### Expected behaviour

I would expect this to compile without issue.

### OS

Linux, RHEL 8.10

### Python version

3.9.17

### Cython version

3.2.4

### Additional context

I do not understand why Cython is looking for a pxd file that isn't referenced.  I tried converting the relative import into an explicit import (`from CythonTest cimport some_c_library`) but that did not change the behavior.  What does seem to work is:

- Convert CythonTest.py to CythonTest.pyx (my current workaround).
- Remove the specification of the include directory (not a great solution for other reasons).
title: [BUG]
